VEGANScreener + metabolomics

Q2+: Associations between VS_total_plus and the metabolome in vegans.

This code is used to explore patterns in the metabolome profiles of vegans, particularly by linking them to the VEGANScreener score + (VS_total_plus).

The script is divided into two parts, according to the integration method of metabolome datasets (see preprocessing scripts for details). At first, the sPCA dataset is analyzed. This dataset is used for multivariate analysis, PCA visualization, PERMANOVA and ML approach. The concatenated dataset of both LCMS and NMR is used for univariate analysis.

The main parts of the analysis is composed of:

  1. Global visualization - 3 components color-coding VS_total_plus

  2. PERMANOVA - how much of the variation can be explained by various external variables: dist(Metabolome) ~ sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + country*VS_total_plus

  3. Univariate analysis (feature-wise model) - linear modelling of metabolite using various external variables: Metabolite ~ sex + bmi + age + veganDuration + Country*VS_total_plus

  4. Random fores (RF) classification - the model is trained and validated through bootstrapping with 500 iterations. The RF model is used to distinguish between participants with 1st and 3rd tercile of VS_total_plus. The two types of models are trained:

  1. ML model on original sPCA dataset
  2. ML model on sPCA dataset after regressing out the covariates.

1 Import

metadata_df <- read.table(
  file =  "../../data/preprocessed/metadata_preprocessed_v3.tsv",
  header = TRUE) %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(VS_total_plus))

lcms_df <- read.table(
  file= "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/lc_ms_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)

nmr_df <- read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/nmr_preprocessed_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)

spca_integrated_df <-  read.table(
  file = "../../data/preprocessed/metabolomics/spca_integrated_vegans_INT_v3.tsv",
  check.names = FALSE) %>% dplyr::filter(rownames(.) %in% metadata_df$study_id)

Scaling - when using columns from metadata as covariates, they need to be scaled, so mean is 0 and SD is 0.

metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_df
metadata_scaled_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)] <- apply(metadata_df[,-c(1,3,5,6,8)],2,scale)
metadata_scaled_df <- metadata_scaled_df %>%
  mutate(sex=as.factor(sex),
         country=as.factor(country),
         group=as.factor(group),
         diet_supplements=as.factor(diet_supplements))

2 Descriptive statistics

Summary of counts per diet group, country and sex:

metadata_df %>% dplyr::group_by(country,sex) %>% count()
# A tibble: 10 × 3
# Groups:   country, sex [10]
   country sex       n
   <chr>   <chr> <int>
 1 BE      F        53
 2 BE      M        28
 3 CH      F        23
 4 CH      M        20
 5 CZ      F        50
 6 CZ      M        41
 7 DE      F        51
 8 DE      M        47
 9 ES      F        28
10 ES      M        15

Basic plots for visualizing the distribution of selected variables:

plot_list <- list()
name <- c("Age (years)","BMI","Vegan diet duration (years)","VS_total(+)")
i <- 1
for (col in c("age","bmi","vegan_duration","VS_total_plus")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) + 
    geom_histogram(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          title = element_text(face = "plain",size=20)) + 
    xlab(name[i]) +
    ggtitle(paste(round(mean(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2),"+-", round(sd(metadata_df[,col],na.rm = TRUE),2)))
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
  i <- i + 1 
                                            
}
i <- 1
name <- c("Sex","Country")
for (col in c("sex","country")){
  p <- ggplot(metadata_df) +
        theme_minimal() + 
     theme(axis.title = element_text(face="bold",size=20),
          axis.text=element_text(face="bold",size=20)) +
    stat_count(aes(x=!!sym(col)),fill="darkblue") + 
  geom_text(data=metadata_df %>% 
              dplyr::group_by(!!sym(col)) %>% count() %>%
              dplyr::mutate(n=n+10), aes(x=!!sym(col),y=n,label=n)) + 
    xlab(name[i]) 
  
  plot_list[[length(plot_list)+1]] <- p
                 
  i <- i+1                           
}

wrap_plots(plot_list)

3 SPCA

The SPCA dataset was created by concatenating LCMS and NMR dataset and applying Mixomics::spca() function. See preprocessing script for details.

mixOmics::spca() performs Sparse Principal Component Analysis, which is similar to regular PCA but adds a sparsity constraint so that only a subset of variables contributes to each component. This sparsity is controlled by penalization, making interpretation easier because irrelevant or noisy variables get loadings of zero.

3.1 Visualization

3.1.1 PCA

spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "VS_total_plus",
              perform_pca = FALSE)

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca <- list()
p_pca[[1]] <- p
png 
  2 
spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p1 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "VS_total_plus",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC2"))

p1 <- ggtheme(p1)

p2 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "VS_total_plus",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC2","PC3"))

p2 <- ggtheme(p2)

p3 <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "VS_total_plus",
              perform_pca = FALSE,axis = c("PC1","PC3"))

p3 <- ggtheme(p3)

p1 + p2 + p3 + 
plot_layout(guides = "collect")

png 
  2

3.1.2 Boxplots

spca_for_uni <- merge(spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

spca_for_uni <- spca_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(VS_total_plus, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_VSplustertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(spca_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
              aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = col,fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p)
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a

png 
  2

3.1.3 Heatmap

metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(VS_total_plus, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_VSplustertile"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-tertile_cut)
VS_colors <- c("1st_VSplustertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_VSplustertile"="orange",
               "3rd_VSplustertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- spca_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=VS_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(VS_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p

png 
  2

Tertile averages

metadata_df %>%
  dplyr::select(VS_total_plus, tertile_label) %>%
  group_by(tertile_label) %>%
  summarise(mean_VSplus = mean(VS_total_plus, na.rm = TRUE))
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  tertile_label     mean_VSplus
  <chr>                   <dbl>
1 1st_VSplustertile        14.1
2 2nd_VSplustertile        22.6
3 3rd_VSplustertile        30.5

3.2 PERMANOVA

The test show how much of the variation in metabolomics is explained by: sex - age - bmi -vegan duration - Country - VS_total_plus

Variables were added sequentially (Type I SS), so each p-value and R² reflects a partial effect, after adjusting for those above. This model accounts for variation due to sex, age, vegan diet duration, BMI and country of origin. Then accounts for the main effects of VS_total. Then tests whether the effect of VS_total varies by country of origin (interaction).

VS_total explains 1.806% of variation after adjusting for sex, age, bmi,country and duration of the diet. This result is statistically significant (p<0.001).

However, the country of origin is still explaining the most of the variation ~ 10%. The interaction effect is not significant, which suggest the differences in VS_total is not inconsistent throughout the countries.

# Create Euclidean distance matrix
dist_mat <- vegdist(spca_integrated_df, 
                    method = "euclidean")

# Test whether vegan vs omnivore differ in metabolomics
adonis_result <- adonis2(
  dist_mat ~ sex + bmi + age + vegan_duration + country*VS_total_plus,
  data = metadata_scaled_df , 
  permutations = 999,
  by="terms")

print(adonis_result)
Permutation test for adonis under reduced model
Terms added sequentially (first to last)
Permutation: free
Number of permutations: 999

adonis2(formula = dist_mat ~ sex + bmi + age + vegan_duration + country * VS_total_plus, data = metadata_scaled_df, permutations = 999, by = "terms")
                       Df SumOfSqs      R2       F Pr(>F)    
sex                     1   1105.2 0.05640 24.1322  0.001 ***
bmi                     1    168.1 0.00858  3.6699  0.001 ***
age                     1    250.9 0.01280  5.4791  0.001 ***
vegan_duration          1     51.7 0.00264  1.1299  0.308    
country                 4   1856.5 0.09473 10.1343  0.001 ***
VS_total_plus           1    352.7 0.01800  7.7016  0.001 ***
country:VS_total_plus   4    149.0 0.00760  0.8133  0.824    
Residual              342  15662.9 0.79925                   
Total                 355  19597.1 1.00000                   
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
adonis_result <- adonis_result[-c(7,9),] %>% rownames_to_column("Var")
adonis_result$Var <- factor(adonis_result$Var,levels = rev(c("sex","bmi","age", "vegan_duration","country","VS_total_plus", "Residual")))
p <- ggplot(adonis_result, aes(fill=Var, x=R2, y="Metabolome")) + 
    geom_bar(position="stack", stat="identity") + 
  ylab("") + xlab("Partial variance explained (R²)") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=rev(c("#ffffcc","#b3cde3","#ccebc5",
                                 "#decbe4","#fed9a6","darkred",
                                 "lightgrey")),name = "Covariate")  

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[2]] <- p
p_pca[[3]] <- adonis_result

3.3 RF model

3.3.1 Original model

sub_metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
dplyr::filter(
  tertile_label %in% c("1st_VSplustertile","3rd_VSplustertile"))

sub_spca_integrated_df <- spca_integrated_df[as.character(sub_metadata_df$study_id),]
model_name <- "VSplus_tertile_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(sub_spca_integrated_df,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                            outcome="Group",
                            N = 500,
                           file = model_name,
                            reuse=TRUE)

roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low VS_total(+)","high VS_total(+)"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c

png 
  2

Accuracy

# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
[1] 0.5106333
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
[1] "Results saved to ../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx"

3.3.2 Adjusted model

# Covariates to adjust for
covars <- c("sex","bmi", "age", "country", "vegan_duration")
integrated_df <- sub_spca_integrated_df
all(rownames(integrated_df) == sub_metadata_df$study_id)
[1] TRUE
adjusted_data <- adjust_for_covars(integrated_df, covars, sub_metadata_df)

model_name <- "adjusted_vstotalplus_model_vegan" 

prepared_df <- model_preparation(adjusted_data,
                                 sub_metadata_df,
                                 predicting_col = "tertile_label")

rf_model <- make_rf_model(data=prepared_df,
                          outcome="Group",
                          N = 500,
                          file = model_name,
                          reuse=TRUE)

rf_model$model_summary
  mtry splitrule min.node.size       auc  accuracy   auc_CIL   auc_CIU
1    3      gini             2 0.6951126 0.6451807 0.6052005 0.7785791
  accuracy_CIL accuracy_CIU
1    0.5465853    0.7317084
roc_c <- roc_curve(model_object = rf_model, 
                   group = c("low VS_total(-)","high VS_total(-)"),CI = TRUE) 

roc_c <- ggtheme(roc_c) + 
  theme(legend.position="none",title = element_text(size=8))

roc_c

png 
  2 
# Find the "best" threshold using Youden's J Index
# This maximizes (Sensitivity + Specificity - 1)
roc_obj <- rf_model$rocobj
best_threshold_data <- coords(roc_obj, "best", 
                              ret = "threshold", 
                              best.method = "youden")
optimal_threshold <- best_threshold_data$threshold
optimal_threshold
[1] 0.509
# saving the results
save_rf_details("../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx",model_name,rf_model)
[1] "Results saved to ../results/vegan/RF_models_performance.xlsx"

3.4 RF importance

3.4.1 Original model

PC5 and PC10 are the most important for RF model.

load("../intermediate_files/models/VSplus_tertile_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))

3.4.2 Adjusted model

Similarly as above, the most important is PC5, with significant drop of importance after this PC.

load("../intermediate_files/models/adjusted_vstotalplus_model_vegan/rf_model.RData")

var_imp <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model$trained_model)) %>%
  rownames_to_column("PC") %>%
  `colnames<-`(c("PC","Importance")) 

var_imp <- var_imp %>% dplyr::mutate(PC=factor(PC,levels = PC[order(var_imp$Importance,decreasing = TRUE)])) 

ggplot(var_imp) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=PC,y=Importance))

3.4.3 Visualization

To explore the important PCs, their visualization is shown below. We will show PC5, as it was consistently picked by two models and PC10 as this PC remained in the list of the most important PCs after adjustment.

spca_plot_df <- merge(
  spca_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
  metadata_df,by="study_id")

p <- pca_plot(spca_integrated_df,
              metadata_df,
              color_col = "VS_total_plus",
              perform_pca = FALSE,
              axis = c("PC5","PC10"))

p <- ggtheme(p)

p

p_pca[[4]] <- p
p <- ggplot(spca_for_uni, 
             aes_string(x = "tertile_label",y = "PC5",fill="tertile_label")) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle("PC5") + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) + 
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(spca_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
p <- ggtheme(p) 

p

png 
  2

3.5 SPCA loadings

Here, we will explore the loadings of the most important PC (PC5)

load("../results/preprocessing/spca_result_vegans_INT_v2.RData")
df <- data.frame (
  Metabolite=names(spca_result$loadings$X[,5]),
  Loading=spca_result$loadings$X[,5])

write.table(df,sep="\t","../results/vegan/Q2_VStotal_plus/loadings_PC5.tsv")

This plot shows the loading of individual metabolites to PC5, ordered by its absolute value.

ggplot(df %>% 
         dplyr::mutate(
           Metabolite=factor(Metabolite,
                             levels = Metabolite[order(abs(Loading),decreasing = FALSE)])
           )) + 
  geom_col(aes(x=Metabolite,y=Loading)) + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=90,hjust = 1)) + 
  coord_flip()


spca_loadings <- df$Metabolite[abs(df$Loading)>=0.1]

4 Concatenated dataset

concat_integrated_df <- cbind(lcms_df,nmr_df)

4.1 Visualization

4.1.1 Boxplots

concat_for_uni <- merge(concat_integrated_df %>% rownames_to_column("study_id"),
                      metadata_scaled_df,by="study_id",all = TRUE) 

concat_for_uni <- concat_for_uni %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(VS_total_plus, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_VSplustertile"
    )
  )

plot_list <- list()
for (col in colnames(concat_integrated_df)){
  p <- ggplot(concat_for_uni, 
              aes(x = tertile_label,
                  y = !!sym(col),
                  fill=tertile_label)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ggtitle(col) + 
    theme_minimal() + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#D73027","orange","#1A9850")) +
    geom_signif(comparisons = 
                  combn(as.character(unique(concat_for_uni$tertile_label)),
                        m=2, simplify = FALSE),
                map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.1,textsize = 2)
  
  p <- ggtheme(p) + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust = 1))
  plot_list[[length(plot_list) + 1]] <- p
}


a <- wrap_plots(plot_list,guides = "collect")

a

png 
  2

4.1.2 Heatmap

metadata_df <- metadata_df %>%
  mutate(
    tertile_cut = ntile(VS_total_plus, 3), # assign 1, 2, or 3 (tertile)
    tertile_label = case_when(                          # label tertiles
      tertile_cut == 1 ~ "1st_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 2 ~ "2nd_VSplustertile",
      tertile_cut == 3 ~ "3rd_VSplustertile"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-tertile_cut)
VS_colors <- c("1st_VSplustertile"="#D73027",
               "2nd_VSplustertile"="orange",
               "3rd_VSplustertile"="#1A9850")

p_df <- concat_integrated_df %>% t()

ann_df <- metadata_df %>% dplyr::select(study_id,tertile_label) %>%
  column_to_rownames("study_id")

ann_colors <- list(
  "tertile_label"=VS_colors
)
names(ann_colors[["tertile_label"]]) <- names(VS_colors)

p <- pheatmap(p_df,cluster_rows = FALSE,show_colnames = FALSE,
              show_rownames = FALSE,
              annotation_col = ann_df,
              annotation_colors = ann_colors,silent = TRUE)

p

png 
  2

4.2 Univariate analysis

We will test the differences between diets and VS total using generalize linear model while adjusting for covariates.

4.2.1 Continuous analysis

Call glm to explore which metabolites are significantly related to VS score while adjusting for age, sex, Country and dietary group:

Metabolite ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country*VS_total_plus

results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_glm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "VS_total_plus"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

results_df$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df$Pr...t..,method = "BH")

Look at significant results - 6 metabolites

results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="VS_total_plus")
          metabolite      variable   Estimate Std..Error   t.value     Pr...t..
1           PA.44.5. VS_total_plus  0.3909323 0.09956441  3.926426 1.042854e-04
2           PC.40.6. VS_total_plus  0.3881690 0.09908414  3.917569 1.080191e-04
3          SM.d42.2. VS_total_plus -0.3128291 0.11128679 -2.811017 5.223104e-03
4       LysoPC.22.6. VS_total_plus  0.4570670 0.10074161  4.537024 7.908634e-06
5 Indoleacrylic.acid VS_total_plus  0.3992800 0.10468628  3.814063 1.621621e-04
6     Acetylornithin VS_total_plus  0.3678134 0.10806272  3.403703 7.437144e-04
   p_adjusted
1 0.001586952
2 0.001597465
3 0.038621542
4 0.000150983
5 0.002310340
6 0.007655883

Check the interaction effect - Methylguanidine

df <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(str_ends(variable, ":VS_total_plus"))

df
       metabolite                variable  Estimate Std..Error  t.value
1 Methylguanidine countryDE:VS_total_plus 0.3539462   0.130019 2.722265
     Pr...t.. p_adjusted
1 0.006815792 0.04708277
pc_to_be_removed <- df$metabolite
concat_significant_df <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="VS_total_plus"))

concat_univariate_results <- results_df

concat_significant <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="VS_total_plus"))$metabolite

# save and show
write.table(concat_significant_df,"../results/vegan/Q2_VStotal_plus/lm_continous.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

knitr::kable(concat_significant)
x
PA.44.5.
PC.40.6.
SM.d42.2.
LysoPC.22.6.
Indoleacrylic.acid
Acetylornithin 
results_all <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="VS_total_plus"))

# save 
write.table(results_all,"../results/vegan/Q2_VStotal_plus/lm_continous_all.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

Volcano plot

vp <- volcano_plot(results_df,comparison = "VS_total_plus")
vp

Correlation

sign <- (results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="VS_total_plus"))$metabolite
df_for_plot <- concat_integrated_df %>% dplyr::select(sign)
corr_object <- cor(concat_for_uni$VS_total,df_for_plot)
corrplot::corrplot(corr_object,method = "number")

Vegan duration

results_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adjusted<0.05) %>%
  dplyr::filter(variable=="vegan_duration")
          metabolite       variable   Estimate Std..Error   t.value    Pr...t..
1          SM.d33.1. vegan_duration -0.1564241 0.05467217 -2.861128 0.004481018
2 Indoleacrylic.acid vegan_duration  0.1468340 0.05389264  2.724566 0.006769495
  p_adjusted
1 0.03432791
2 0.04707265

4.2.2 Pairwise analysis

Metab ~ sex + bmi + age + eq_vegan_dur_total + Country + tertile_label

LM

results_list <- lapply(colnames(concat_integrated_df), 
                       function(x) fit_netglm(x,concat_for_uni,
                                           tested_variable = "tertile_label"))
results_df <- do.call(rbind, results_list)

vs_effects <- results_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(`p.value`, method = "BH"))

concat_significant_cat <-  vs_effects %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05)

# save and show
write.table(concat_significant_cat,"../results/vegan/Q2_VStotal_plus/lm_cat.tsv",
            row.names = FALSE,
            quote=FALSE,
            sep="\t")

knitr::kable(concat_significant_cat)
metabolite tertile_label emmean p.value lower.CL upper.CL p_adj
PA.44.5. 1st_VSplustertile -0.4597119 0.0000001 -0.6275408 -0.2918830 0.0000298

Acetylornithin 1st_VSplustertile -0.3503191 0.0001662 -0.5312900 -0.1693482 0.0041541
Acetylornithin 3rd_VSplustertile 0.3285842 0.0005885 0.1422835 0.5148850 0.0120384
Formate 1st_VSplustertile 0.3060344 0.0000033 0.1787045 0.4333643 0.0001524
